claim: Loop engineering (using /loop, /goal, /routines in Claude Code) is not inherently superior to manual prompting; it trades control for autonomy and is expensive in tokens
actionable: Treat loop engineering as a specialization for specific use cases (autonomous GitHub issue handling, repetitive tasks), not as a universal replacement for direct prompting
source_title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore

claim: Loop-based orchestrators must reason multiple times per cycle (init planning, worker prompting, result aggregation, next-cycle planning), making them token-expensive; a single workflow can consume >1M tokens
actionable: When evaluating loop-based systems, budget token costs assuming 3-5 reasoning passes per orchestration cycle; use smaller models (Kimi K2.7) for orchestration, reserve frontier models for implementation
source_title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore

claim: /loop in Claude Code runs within a single session; extended loops bloat context and overwhelm the LLM, requiring separate agent sessions for isolation and determinism
actionable: For multi-turn loop workflows, use separate Claude Code sessions per logical phase (planning, implementation, review) or harness tools like Archon that distribute work across sessions
source_title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore

claim: Deterministic workflow harnesses (like Archon) provide better reliability than pure loop engineering by explicitly defining steps and decision points rather than leaving orchestration to the LLM
actionable: When building multi-agent workflows, use harnesses that combine deterministic process definition with LLM reasoning at specific steps (e.g., decide bug vs feature, then branch)
source_title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore

claim: Using different models at different workflow stages (e.g., Kimi for orchestration, Claude Code for implementation, Codex for review) optimizes cost without sacrificing quality
actionable: Design multi-phase workflows where you explicitly select model per stage: use small models for orchestration/classification, frontier models for implementation, efficient models for validation
source_title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore

claim: Observability is critical for reliable loops: dashboards tracking orchestrator decisions, worker outputs, and state transitions enable cost optimization and debugging
actionable: Build observability infrastructure for multi-agent systems: dashboard + database to log all orchestrator decisions, token usage per step, and worker outputs
source_title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore

claim: Orchestrator-worker systems that store state in external databases enable workflow resumption after failures or interruptions, providing durability that in-session-only loops lack
actionable: When implementing loop-like workflows, store execution state (what was completed, what remains) in an external database that the orchestrator reads at each cycle
source_title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore

claim: Human-in-the-loop checkpoints in multi-agent workflows prevent hallucination compounding by allowing validation before proceeding to next orchestration cycle
actionable: Add pause points in loop-based workflows where humans can review orchestrator decisions and worker outputs before allowing the next round of autonomous work
source_title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore

claim: Retool enables rapid deployment of dashboards for multi-agent observability; React-based UI code can be generated by Claude Code and deployed without custom backend work
actionable: Use Retool for deploying observability dashboards: have Claude Code generate React code, import it into Retool, connect to your Postgres backend with AI-assisted wiring
source_title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore
